reasoning: The unsafe findings are false positives; flagged scripts aren't executed during makepkg build/install. They're unused test scripts irrelevant to the generic architecture build.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged test scripts (picorv32.sh, picorv32_benchmark.py) as unsafe due to external downloads, but these aren't invoked during build/install.
coverage_issues: DeepSeek missed picorv32.sh/picorv32_benchmark.py in file selection
Neither model verified if scripts are actually executed during PKGBUILD-defined build steps
false_positives: picorv32.sh: Test script not invoked during build (ice40-specific, unused in generic build)
picororv32_benchmark.py: Benchmark tool not referenced in PKGBUILD functions
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Weight files by PKGBUILD references and build context (e.g., ignore arch-specific tools when -DARCH=generic is set)
Enhance prompt: Explicitly require verification of script execution context during build/install phases
Add build-context analysis: Cross-reference CMake flags/PKGBUILD variables to filter irrelevant files
